translationText: Woe to the defrauders.

arabicText: ٱلَّذِينَ إِذَا ٱكْتَالُوا۟ عَلَى ٱلنَّاسِ يَسْتَوْفُونَ
translationText: Those who, when they take a measure from people, they take in full.
But when they measure or weigh to others, they cheat.
Do these not know that they will be resurrected?
For a Great Day?
The Day when mankind will stand before the Lord of the Worlds?

arabicText: كَلَّآ إِنَّ كِتَٰبَ ٱلْفُجَّارِ لَفِى سِجِّينٍۢ
translationText: Not at all. The record of the wicked is in Sijjeen.
But how can you know what Sijjeen is?
A numerical book.
Woe on that Day to the deniers.
Those who deny the Day of Reckoning.

arabicText: وَمَا يُكَذِّبُ بِهِۦٓ إِلَّا كُلُّ مُعْتَدٍ أَثِيمٍ
translationText: But none denies it except the sinful aggressor.

arabicText: إِذَا تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِ ءَايَٰتُنَا قَالَ أَسَٰطِيرُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ
translationText: When Our revelations are recited to him, he says, "Legends of the ancients."

arabicText: كَلَّا ۖ بَلْ ۜ رَانَ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِم مَّا كَانُوا۟ يَكْسِبُونَ
translationText: Not at all. Their hearts have become corroded by what they used to earn.

arabicText: كَلَّآ إِنَّهُمْ عَن رَّبِّهِمْ يَوْمَئِذٍۢ لَّمَحْجُوبُونَ
translationText: Not at all. On that Day, they will be screened from their Lord.
Then they will roast in Hell.

arabicText: ثُمَّ يُقَالُ هَٰذَا ٱلَّذِى كُنتُم بِهِۦ تُكَذِّبُونَ
translationText: Then it will be said, "This is what you used to deny."

arabicText: كَلَّآ إِنَّ كِتَٰبَ ٱلْأَبْرَارِ لَفِى عِلِّيِّينَ
translationText: No indeed; the record of the righteous is in Elliyyeen.
But how can you know what Elliyyoon is?
A numerical book.
Witnessed by those brought near.
Indeed, the righteous will be amid bliss.
On thrones, looking on.
You will recognize on their faces the radiance of bliss.
They will be given to drink a sealed wine.

arabicText: خِتَٰمُهُۥ مِسْكٌۭ ۚ وَفِى ذَٰلِكَ فَلْيَتَنَافَسِ ٱلْمُتَنَٰفِسُونَ
translationText: Whose seal is musk-this is what competitors should compete for.
Its mixture is of Tasneem.
A spring from which those brought near drink.

arabicText: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ أَجْرَمُوا۟ كَانُوا۟ مِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ يَضْحَكُونَ
translationText: Those who committed crimes used to laugh at those who believed.
And when they passed by them, they would wink at one another.

arabicText: وَإِذَا ٱنقَلَبُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰٓ أَهْلِهِمُ ٱنقَلَبُوا۟ فَكِهِينَ
translationText: And when they went back to their families, they would go back exulting.

arabicText: وَإِذَا رَأَوْهُمْ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّ هَٰٓؤُلَآءِ لَضَآلُّونَ
translationText: And if they saw them, they would say, "These people are lost."
Yet they were not sent as guardians over them.

arabicText: فَٱلْيَوْمَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مِنَ ٱلْكُفَّارِ يَضْحَكُونَ
translationText: But on that Day, those who believed will laugh at the unbelievers.
On luxurious furnishings, looking on.

arabicText: هَلْ ثُوِّبَ ٱلْكُفَّارُ مَا كَانُوا۟ يَفْعَلُونَ
translationText: Have the unbelievers been repaid for what they used to do?
